definition.name: Radon_Nikodym_for_states
definition.definition: (Radon-Nikodym type theorem for states). Let φ be a state on a unital *-algebra A with GNS triple (π_φ, H_φ, ξ_φ). If ψ is a positive linear functional on A with ψ ≤ λφ (meaning λφ - ψ is positive) for some λ > 0, then there exists a unique operator T ∈ π_φ(A)' (in the commutant) with 0 ≤ T ≤ λI such that ψ(a) = ⟨π_φ(a)Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ for all a ∈ A. This map ψ ↦ T establishes a bijection between positive functionals dominated by φ (up to scaling) and positive elements of the commutant, and is the key ingredient in proving the Alfsen-Shultz face-commutant correspondence. The result is analogous to the classical Radon-Nikodym theorem for measures (absolutely continuous measures correspond to L^1 densities). Reference: Alfsen-Shultz, State Spaces of Operator Algebras (2001), Prop 5.31; Takesaki, Theory of Operator Algebras I, §I.9.
